transcript.text: This meeting is being recorded.
Thank you.
Hi, Jill.
Hey there.
How's it going?
Good, good.
Happy New Year.
Happy New Year.
Yeah, thank you.
Likewise.
How were your holidays?
Hope it was restful.
It was good.
Yeah, I was fortunate to take the last couple of weeks off.
think you took some time off too, right?
Yeah, yeah.
It was, it was what I needed.
Nice.
Good to hear.
Yeah, I know you said Jason will not be joining.
So let me share my screen and get started.
All right.
So, yeah, just as a brief recap, we're pausing this weekend next just to sort of clear up the review queue.
And we've also been editing some of the older articles just for format.
All right.
All
I had proposed some programmatic pages, I know you saw those already, just pasted it in here just for reference, but yeah, key to hear what you think when you get around to it.
The idea here is just that these pages, so there's a couple things we're trying to achieve with this.

One is that since the pages will be pretty templated, we hope that in terms of legal review, the team just has to review the templates themselves, and then we can probably just scale the rest of it, so the actual review load will be reduced, and we can increase our publishing base.

The other thing is that from our end, of course, if the page is templated, it's super easy to build a pipeline to it, and, you know, we can even scale volumes if needed, so that's kind of the thinking behind this, so yeah, keen to hear your thoughts on it whenever you get the chance to review it.
We conducted a monthly performance.
Review analysis for December, found some content recommendations, et cetera, which I'll go into in deeper detail.
But just as a summary, you know, we are suggesting some content to close LLM visibility gaps to the competition.
And there were some interesting local SEO type searches that came up.
So I just wanted to run all of that past you.

So, yeah, diving into performance, basically, in terms of overall sessions for December, given that, you know, two weeks were basically holiday weeks, we came pretty, we outpaced November, came pretty close to October as well.
So generally, that was quite encouraging.
And of course, you know, clearing the publishing backlog, I'm sure, in January will help us hit even higher levels, because generally the content seems to be doing quite well.
So the knowledge center as well, traffic was pretty steady throughout,
Again, December traffic was pretty good to the Knowledge Center in general.
Diving into the keyword analysis.
So for positions, the top position, we started December with about 1,943-odd keywords.
We finished with 1,700, which obviously is going the wrong way there.
And in terms of positions 2 to 10, we started with 1,700-odd, finished with 1,500.
But something to point out about this data is that this is a raw export from Google Search Console.
And the GSC usually includes a lot of long-tail keywords that are not quite relevant.
For example, there'll be keywords that are like 10 to 15 words long, right, because people literally typed it into Google.
So it's just a raw data dump.
In terms of the core keywords that we're interested in, we didn't see any major losses, which is why we're not flagging anything as a red flag over here.
Something interesting I did see was that...
Sunbit is receiving a lot of impressions for, especially the shop directory, is receiving a lot of impressions for these kinds of searches, which is, you know, let me see if I can zoom in.
There we go.
You know, not dental office for sale, obviously, but, you know, something like break shop near me, auto repair shop, auto shops near me, and so on.
All these near me kinds of searches.
And the shop directory is receiving impressions, but nobody's thinking into it.
So, you know, the overall ranking is low.
I wanted to check with you, is local SEO a priority at all for you, or are you just not interested in it generally?
Um, I mean, I would say, yeah, I think that yes, I think it is becoming a priority.
Um, I know last year we've spent a lot of time with sales the H H
You know, our kind of our website agency to start help, to help us start cleaning up and just kind of optimizing for SEO.
But obviously, like we have been thinking, you know, in the last six months and until this time that, you know, we're planning for that rebrand for Sunbit.
So, you know, that has been a prioritization as far as kind of just the website in general, as well as the Sunbit.ai stuff.
So with that being said, like with that understanding, like we know that, you know, we're going to see some declines.
We're going to see, you know, some negative data points as we launch or roll out that rebrand mid-year.
The goal is still June, July for that launch of the rebrand.
So, you know, essentially, like it's an entirely new brand.
We're going to be updating a lot of websites.
The the look, the feel, everything from top to bottom, front to back, right?
So I know that like years prior to last year, SEO was just kind of like at the back of our minds.
Like we were aware of it.
We were doing what we could at the bare minimum.
Last year, we kind of stepped it up a little bit and wanted to kind of look at more ways to optimize.

And this year, I know that, again, first half of year is really dedicated to the rebrand and to the Sunbit AI, but also keeping SEO, you know, at the forefront, just making sure that anything we do is, you know, a positive change and just minimal disruption to what we can control.
So I would say yes, you know, like we're open to ideas, of course, you know, I think, yeah, I think this is kind of interesting, right?
Like the impressions of the shop directory, but nobody clicking on it because like on our end, I think.
I we see, too, that the shop directory actually does get a lot of traffic and we have quite a bit of conversions from it.
So, yeah, kind of curious, you know, I'd be curious to see what Jason's thought would be as well.
Yeah, yeah, it's interesting because the shop directory is pretty healthy.
So this graph kind of shows, like, the trends in general.
So we've been kind of in a holding trend for the past few months here.
Yeah, that was one of the reasons why I wanted to flag these because a lot of these near-me searches, the shop directory as it is designed right now, it's not quite optimized for these searches.
One way to optimize it, and there's a few different ways we can go here depending on how you wish to prioritize it.
One is we can just launch so we can add it to the programmatic ideas that I had proposed, essentially creating pages like, you know, best dental service providers, for example, in location.

So whichever location is maybe a high priority for you, where you're seeing a lot of customers come from, where you have a lot of merchants, et cetera, we create individual pages for those locations and then list these people in there, have SEO elements in there, obviously, and then link to the shop directory or just treat them as, you know, standalone conversion pages.
So that would be one way to take advantage of these near-knee searches over here.
The other thing is your partners.
Let's say we don't want to do the programmatic stuff.
One way of doing it would be asking your partners to mention Sunbit on their pages and in their Google My Business profiles.
That kind of lends more credibility to the shop directory page as well.
So that could be one way of kind of, you know, getting some clicks from some of these searches.
And, yeah, generally, you know, programmatic pages would be the optimal way of making use of this.
And we can draw from the shop directory collection that you have in your CMS, obviously, to populate that data.
But it's just something to think about.
I wanted to flag this because it seems like there's quite a lot of opportunity for this.
It seems like these kinds of searches are the ones we want to show up for.
And, yeah, just wanted to put it out there that, you know, this is something else that we can do in terms of a programmatic opportunity over here.
Okay.
Yeah.
So, yeah.
As I said, the shop directory reviews are kind of in this little range over here, but still pretty healthy.
And in terms of LLM performance, you know, it declined, obviously, in December.
This is for the entire website.
Referral traffic declined, but that was kind of expected given that we had kind of two weeks off.
So just going to wait and see, like, how JAN plays out, and then we can, you know, see what we can do.
For the queries about tracking in Scrub.
Generally, Sunbit is in a healthy position.
CareCredit is way, way ahead of everybody else.
So obviously, we need to do some work to catch up to them.
But in terms of the gap to Cherry, we actually got better.
So over the course of the month, we increased this percentage of top positions.
Sentiment is overwhelmingly positive.
And also the citations are kind of low at 2%, but then competitors put together are at 9%.
So this is mainly a function of CareCredit capturing a lot of the citation traffic.
We're not seeing Cherry show up a lot in citations.
So generally, the numbers look pretty healthy with the caveat that, yes, of course, we do need to sort of close the gap.
So with that in mind, that's where these topics come in.
So what we did is we basically pulled the queries where, you know, we're going things.

CareCredit is showing up, but we aren't, and that's what this list is, and as you can see, there's about, you know, 98 of these prompts where we're not showing up in ChatGPG, and what we did is we kind of categorized them much like you would categorize keywords that just came up with, and the thing is, right now what we're doing is for these topics, we're looking at existing content that we have, and the aim is to basically enrich or refresh the content with these queries that, you know, are kind of missing, essentially.
So, if, let's say there's no existing content, we plan on just creating a new page for it with these queries in there, so that we can sort of reduce that gap.
So, that's the, that's the plan for the next couple weeks, and obviously, we still have the topics in queue that you keep producing once, you know, we're out of the two-week period.
But yeah, that's kind of the, you know, summary of performance over the past month.
Any questions or anything I can answer here?
So just to confirm that with this, these queries, if the content does exist, are you all going in and re-pushing the updated article copy into, you know, the published landing page for that article?
Or are we needing to do it?
How is that?
Um, we, we can do, would it all be easier for you?
Because I was wondering, like, does it need another review from compliance?
Or how is that?
Hmm, I guess that's, yeah, I guess, let me answer your question with another question.
How, like, how much of the copy do you anticipate, uh, is being updated?
Yeah, it, it depends.
Um, it's.
It's not a, it depends really, like some of these articles, for example, like some of these have just like one query, or let's say this one, right?
Here, there's only like one query that's missing that we're not showing up for.
So maybe it's going to be like maybe a sentence or maybe one FAQ or something like that.
But some of these have like multiple queries that we kind of need to update.
So it varies.
So I actually want to ask you like, what would be the best way of doing this?
Because I don't want to increase the review sort of burden on you guys.
So yeah, just, just wanted to maybe brainstorm like a good way to do this, the least painful way, I suppose.
Mm-hmm.
How much time do you anticipate needing to go through?
How many was it about like?
Yeah, this is about, uh, 39.
Okay.

Okay, so like with 39 articles to basically audit all the content, see if we, if you need to create new versus what we already have that can just be updated or edited, modified, whatever, like how much time do you anticipate needing to kind of diagnose that?
We should get it done by the end of next week.
Okay, that's good.
So why don't we put this as like, let's circle back.
And I'm supposed to meet with Jason tomorrow, as long as he's not flying.
I think, I think I'm supposed to, so I will kind of mention that to him.
So it'll, you know, and then if you want to reiterate that in the recap in our Slack, that will be helpful too.
I think what will be helpful is just kind of having like a pretty consolidated list of things that we need to like, make sure we're talking about here internally before or ahead of when we
Meet Next Week with Jason, just so he has a good, you know, like a comprehensive summary of what we talked about today.
And he and I can kind of like go through it together during our one-on-one.
Yeah, makes sense.
Okay.
Also, just want to say that at some point, we might need to come up with a general workflow of how to handle refreshes.
Because, you know, as the published content volume increases, refreshes become more and more important, especially for LLM visibility.
So given that there's a, you know, compliance step over here, I just want to flag that we might want to start thinking about like, okay, how do we want to do this?
Is there something we should avoid during refreshes so that we don't get stuck in compliance or, you know, how that would work for you as well?
So just want to flag that as well.
It's not an urgent thing, but as the months go on, I think.
So it's definitely something that we probably want to look at process-wise.
So I guess I'm not following.
Are you saying like as we come up?

Yeah, so as we publish more content essentially, and since we're publishing large volumes, just to maintain performance and to also ensure that we're consistently showing up in LLMs, we want to keep refreshing our content, just making sure that it stays towards the top.

Since with the refreshes and with the content publishing that is that compliance review step, which kind of takes some time, we might want to just like brainstorm, okay, what is the best way of refreshing our content so that it doesn't get stuck in compliance for far too long?
Or, you know, what is the easiest way of doing it, et cetera?
Might be worth a discussion with Jason and, you know, the two of us.
Just to, like, figure out how that process would work.
Just want to flag it.
Yeah.
Yep.
That sounds good.
That sounds good.
All right.
That was pretty much it.
So I'll send all of this in the call recap so that, you know, Jason has full context as well, and that, you know, you can sort of bring that up to him as well in an easy way.
That's pretty much it in terms of the updates.
There's anything else I can answer or clarify?
No.
This is good.
Cool.
And an update about the Sunbit AI domain.
We are working on an AEO strategy for that.
We should have something to present to you before the end of the month.
So our director team is kind of working on it, and we'll have something to present by the end of June.
Okay.
Awesome.
Thank you.
All right.
Thanks, Angel.
Bye.
Bye.
Bye.
